name: Laguna de la Nava Loop
name_original: Laguna de la Nava – circular desde Fuentes de Nava
description: Cycle the 12.5-mile (20.1 km) difficult Laguna de la Nava circular route from Fuentes de Nava, exploring wetlands and historic canals.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Campos are primarily characterized by the flat, agricultural landscapes of the Tierra de Campos region in Palencia, Spain. The area features extensive plains, river valleys, and the historic Canal de Castilla, which offers level paths suitable for cycling. Elevation changes are generally gentle, with most routes involving only minor hills. This terrain provides a network of paths and quiet roads, ideal for extended touring cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Campos The most popular touring cycling route is Parque de Valdellano – circular desde Palencia por Villamartín de Campos, a 23.2 miles (37.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle ascents and passes through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Canal de Castilla – circular desde Palencia por Monzón de Campos, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route follows sections of the historic Canal de Castilla, offering flat terrain and views of the surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Canal de Castilla – circular desde Fròmista por Osorno, a 25.8 miles (41.6 km) trail leading through the flat plains and alongside the Canal de Castilla, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Touring cycling around Campos is defined by the Canal de Castilla, agricultural plains, and quiet rural roads. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 1000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Campos?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes around Campos listed on komoot. These routes offer a variety of experiences across the flat, agricultural landscapes of the Tierra de Campos region.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Campos?
A: The routes in Campos cater to various skill levels. You'll find approximately 15 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 13 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners and experienced cyclists alike.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Campos?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Campos are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1143803145/parque-de-valdellano-circular-desde-palencia-por-villamartin-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Valdellano – circular desde Palencia por Villamartín de Campos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175507213/canal-de-castilla-circular-desde-palencia-por-monzon-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla – circular desde Palencia por Monzón de Campos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while cycling in Campos?
A: Touring cycling in Campos is characterized by extensive plains, river valleys, and the historic Canal de Castilla. The terrain is generally flat with gentle elevation changes, offering quiet roads and paths through rural and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What interesting sights can I see along the touring cycling routes in Campos?
A: Along the routes, you can explore various points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major feature, with highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6789295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Statue in Carrión de los Condes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas de Moratinos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks to explore while cycling in Campos?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can cycle past several castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota of Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175190519/the-fortifications-of-tierra-de-campos-circular-from-ampudia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The fortifications of Tierra de Campos – circular from Ampudia&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these historical structures.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Campos?
A: Absolutely. About 15 of the routes are classified as easy. An excellent option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175182934/french-way-and-ucieza-river-loop-from-villalcazar-de-sirga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way and Ucieza River Loop from Villalcázar de Sirga&lt;/a&gt;, which is 20.3 miles (32.7 km) long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Campos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the flat terrain, the scenic views along the Canal de Castilla, and the quiet rural roads that make for a pleasant touring experience.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the Canal de Castilla?
A: The Canal de Castilla is a defining feature of touring cycling in Campos. Many routes incorporate sections of this historic waterway, offering flat and picturesque paths. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170888784/canal-de-castilla-circular-desde-fromista-por-osorno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla – circular desde Fròmista por Osorno&lt;/a&gt; for a dedicated experience along the canal.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for more experienced cyclists?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175143288/via-aquitania-circular-desde-carrion-de-los-condes-ev3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Aquitania – circular desde Carrión de los Condes (EV3)&lt;/a&gt; cover over 43 kilometers (26.7 miles) and offer a moderate challenge. While the terrain is generally flat, the distance provides a good workout.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in Campos?
A: The duration varies depending on the route and your pace. Most moderate routes, which are typically between 30-40 kilometers (18-25 miles), can be completed in about 2.5 to 3.5 hours. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170915540/tamara-de-campos-y-santoyo-circular-desde-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Támara de Campos y Santoyo – circular desde Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; takes around 2 hours 47 minutes.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Tierra de Campos region's unique features?
A: Yes, many routes are designed to showcase the distinct characteristics of the Tierra de Campos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170901608/monzon-de-campos-castle-and-canal-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle and Canal de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, combining historical sites with the natural beauty of the canal and surrounding plains.
tips: Natural wonder located on the outskirts of Nava de las Fuentes. With a large area, this lagoon is a refuge for numerous migratory birds and a habitat for rich biodiversity. Its calm waters and picturesque surroundings make it a perfect place for bird watching and to enjoy nature in its purest form.
Built throughout the fifteenth and sixteenth centuries. The late-Gothic church has three naves separated by pillars and a 63-metre-high tower. This masterpiece is also called "Estrella de Campos".

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Laguna de la Nava – circular desde Fuentes de Nava route?
faqs.answer: Despite being listed as 'difficult' in some classifications, this route is generally considered easy and not physically demanding. It's ideal for beginners and families, offering a relaxed touring cycling experience. The terrain is mostly flat, following unpaved roads and tracks, with some asphalt sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows unpaved roads and tracks, often referred to as greenways, with some sections of asphalt. The profile is notably flat, especially along the Canal de Castilla, making for an easy and enjoyable ride.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary natural feature is the Laguna de la Nava, an internationally important wetland known for its abundant birdlife. You'll also cycle along sections of the historic Canal de Castilla, featuring 18th and 19th-century engineering marvels like locks and bridges. In Fuentes de Nava, you can see historical architecture, including the Church of San Pedro de Fuentes de Nava.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna de la Nava – circular desde Fuentes de Nava route suitable for families and beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This route is described as ideal for beginners and families due to its relaxed pace, easy terrain, and minimal elevation changes. It's a great way to enjoy nature and history together.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route and observe wildlife?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit for cycling and birdwatching is typically during migration seasons (spring and autumn) when the Laguna de la Nava is bustling with diverse bird species. However, the route offers pleasant cycling conditions throughout much of the year, weather permitting.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Laguna de la Nava cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs within the protected areas of the Laguna de la Nava wetland may apply, especially concerning wildlife disturbance. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they do not disturb the birds or other wildlife, particularly near observation points.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the Laguna de la Nava or the cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the public paths around the Laguna de la Nava or along the Canal de Castilla. However, some specific facilities like visitor centers or bird hides might have their own access rules or suggested donations.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of Fuentes de Nava, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for this circular route. Look for designated parking areas within the town.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The Laguna de la Nava is a significant ecological site, home to 222 recorded bird species, representing 41% of all species in Spain. You can expect to see a wide variety of waterfowl and migratory birds, especially from designated observation huts. The surrounding plains may also offer glimpses of other local fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options in Fuentes de Nava?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fuentes de Nava is a town with local amenities. You can find cafes and pubs for refreshments, and there are typically accommodation options such as rural guesthouses or small hotels available for those wishing to extend their stay near the Laguna de la Nava.

faqs.question: Are there specific viewpoints or bird hides along the Laguna de la Nava section?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Laguna de la Nava is equipped with designated observation huts and viewpoints specifically designed for birdwatching. These allow you to get closer to the wildlife without disturbing them, offering excellent opportunities to observe the diverse avian population.

waypoints.name: Church of San Pedro de Fuentes de Nava
